course_title: Honours Research Project in Veterinary Technology
course_description:
This course allows veterinary technicians to undertake their own research project under the supervision of an experienced academic. With the help of their supervisor, students will design a study, collect and analyse data and write up their study as a small thesis. They will also present their study as a seminar to their peers and academics. This will provide the student with an appreciation of the research process and the concept of evidence-based medicine that underpins clinical practice. Students also receive training in research skills that enables them to pursue further research study.

Students commencing in Semester 1 full-time enrol in VETS6631 for 2 consecutive semesters. Students commencing in Semester 2 full-time enrol in VETS6632 for 2 consecutive semesters.

In Honours Research Projects, there are no formal lectures. You are expected to work on your research project and prepare drafts of your thesis and seminars under the guidance of your supervisor(s). The research project extends over two semesters full-time and is written up as a small thesis that includes an abstract, an introduction, a review of the relevant literature, a description of the methodology used, documentation of experimental results, a discussion of experimental results with respect to other published findings, a conclusion and a list of references used.

restrictions: Enrolment restricted to Bachelor of Veterinary Technology (Honours) students.

timetable:
Additional timetable information
The work plan should be an agreed-upon arrangement between the student and the supervisor.
All students will present a
project proposal
seminar
early in the first semester of their enrolment
Students will present a
final seminar
immediately after the submission of their thesis (November).
It is required that all students attend each other's seminars.
course_aims: The general aim of this postgraduate research program is that students complete theses of merit. This course should encourage you to develop and demonstrate capabilities for critical thought and independent research. You will learn how to conceive, design, carry out and communicate science through the development and practice of sound research skills together with the presentation of your results.

learning_outcomes.description: Have the ability to perform self-directed, original and independent research
Display skills in time management, problem solving and organisation
Have a sound understanding of the selected field of research and highlight areas which require further research and innovation
Have the ability to critically evaluate other studies in this area of research as well as critically evaluate and highlight any problems in this study
Display skills in experimental design and appropriate statistical analysis and able to draw valid conclusions from the analysis
Convey and communicate ideas in a number of different ways (verbal and written)
Be able to work as part of a functioning laboratory and part of a university system and be able to work effectively with others as part of a cohesive research team

assessment_details.title: Learning Contract
assessment_details.due_date: The Learning Contract is to be submitted at the time of the Project Proposal Seminar
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students are required to submit a Learning Contract at the time of the Project Proposal Seminar. A Learning Contract template can be obtained from the Course Blackboard site. The Learning Contract is a Pass/Fail assessment item. This document will be a collaboration between the supervisor(s) and the student within four weeks of commencing study. The purpose of this document is to ensure, where possible, that the following aspects are addressed before the Census Date: The project has been considered; The supervisor(s)/student responsibilities and study plan have been discussed and agreed upon; Research integrity, budgets, compliance with UQ policies, compliance with external regulatory bodies and funding opportunities have been discussed and understood; Due diligence is completed; this has been included to ensure the student has every opportunity to succeed in this program. This is a Pass/Fail item, based on submission of the completed form. Students who are unable to submit the form by the due date will be recommended to withdraw from the program before the census date. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission, independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description

Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students with acceptable reasons (medical or compassionate reasons or as a result of exceptional circumstances) may apply for an extension of the deadline. Information on applying for an extension can be found here: my.UQ Applying for an extension .
Extension applications must be received by the assessment due date and time.

assessment_details.title: Honours Seminar
assessment_details.due_date: 2/11/2026 - 20/11/2026 A mutually agreeable date will be organised between the student, supervisor(s) and course coordinator for the presentation of the Honours Seminar.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: 20-25 min oral presentation of the student's Honours research project (in-person, Gatton campus) Seminars should follow the structure of a standard 30min research seminar, with a 20-25min presentation followed by questions from the panel for 5-10 minutes. Students should seek detailed guidance from their supervisor (s) for the development and review of their seminar. Students must prepare a seminar abstract suitable for advertising the seminar throughout SVS, have this reviewed by their advisors, and provide the abstract to the course coordinator 2 weeks before their agreed presentation date. See the SVS BVetTech Honours handbook on the course Blackboard site for further information. A template for the seminar is also available on Blackboard. This honours seminar is worth 25% of the assessment, and it will be assessed by the thesis examiners or others as appointed by the course coordinator. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of the assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission, independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
This will be an in-person presentation on the Gatton Campus, with an abstract due to the course coordinator TWO (2) weeks before the agreed presentation date.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to defer this exam .
Students must be available for their examinations on the scheduled date, time, and campus of their course enrolment. The exam format is determined by the Course Coordinator and may include written (paper-based, online, or digital), practical, oral, or other timed assessments (such as quizzes).
If you are unable to sit for your original examination, you may be eligible for a deferred examination. To apply, you must demonstrate that unavoidable circumstances prevented you from attending your exam. If this is not possible, you can apply for a one-off discretionary deferred exam. All requests for deferred examinations MUST include supporting documentation. For more information, including acceptable evidence and application instructions, please visit my.UQ Deferring an Exam .
Please note that there are no provisions to defer an already-deferred exam. You must be available to sit your deferred examination at the allocated time.
Students who submit five or more deferral requests within a twelve-month period will be contacted with advice on the services and support available within the University.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam .

assessment_details.title: Honours Thesis
assessment_details.due_date: 30/10/2026 2:00 pm Thesis will be submitted by 2pm of the final day of the second semester of study.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Time limited. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students will submit a final Honours thesis. The thesis will include an abstract, introduction (detailing previous work and the aims and hypotheses of the current study), methodology, results, discussion (including conclusions) and references. Details on the format and content requirements of the honours thesis are available on the course Blackboard and in the SVS BVetTech honours handbook. The final thesis will be examined by two examiners – normally, this will be conducted by two internal examiners or one internal examiner and one external to the School of Veterinary Science (SVS). The percentage mark awarded will be the average of the two examiners' marks. Thesis marks will be subject to a moderation process conducted by the course coordinator or a discipline expert nominated by the course coordinator if they differ by 10% or more. A third examiner will be used and the final thesis mark an average of the three marks. A mark of no less than 50% in the honours thesis is required to pass the course. An overall mark of no less than 50% is required to pass the course. Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
When you are satisfied that you can make no further improvements in the quality of your thesis, then it is time to submit your thesis and this will be through the TURNITIN system and a link will be provided near to the due date.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students with acceptable reasons (medical or compassionate reasons or as a result of exceptional circumstances) may apply for an extension of the deadline. Information on applying for an extension can be found here: my.UQ Applying for an extension .
Extension applications must be received by the assessment due date and time.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
You are required to submit assessable items on time.
If you fail to meet the submission deadline for any assessment item, then 10% of the maximum possible mark for the assessment item (assessment ‘marked from’ value) will be deducted as a late penalty for every day (or part day) late after the due date.
For example, if you submit your assignment 1 hour late, you will be penalised 10%; if your assignment is 24.5 hours late, you will be penalised 20% (because it is late by one 24-hour period plus part of another 24-hour period).
10% will be deducted per day for up to 7 calendar days, at which point your submission will receive a mark of zero (0) unless an extension has been approved.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.title: Supervisor Evaluation
assessment_details.due_date: 30/10/2026 2:00 pm Supervisor Evaluation will be submitted by the Primary Supervisor by the thesis due date.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Secure. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Supervisor(s) will grade students according to their engagement in the research project. This document should be reviewed collaboratively by the supervisor and student at the start of candidature, so both parties understand what will be assessed during the course. The supervisor(s) mark is an objective assessment of the overall quality of the student in terms of their scholarship, practical competence and professional approach to research. The supervisor evaluation form is available on Blackboard. The principal supervisor must send the completed evaluations to the course coordinator via email by the due date. Where a student has multiple supervisors who do not agree on a single evaluation, supervisors may submit their evaluations separately to the course coordinator. An average mark from all advisory inputs will be used for grading. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Supervisor Evaluation will be submitted by the Primary Supervisor
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Students with acceptable reasons (medical or compassionate reasons or as a result of exceptional circumstances) may apply for an extension of the deadline. Information on applying for an extension can be found here: my.UQ Applying for an extension .
Extension applications must be received by the assessment due date and time.

learning_activities.topic: Demonstrate self directed learning Learning outcomes: L01, L02
Review and critique published scientific literature Learning outcomes: L03, L04
Devise, plan and conduct an Honours research project Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
Develop research literacy through participation in research seminars Learning outcomes: L06
Use online resources as part of research Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
Work in a laboratory environment (optional) Learning outcomes: L01
Collect field based data (optional) Learning outcomes: L01

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments for Students Policy and Procedure AI for Assessment Guide Recording of Teaching Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Occupational Health and Safety in the Laboratory Guideline Fitness to Practise Policy Vaccinations and Immunisation Procedure Work Integrated Learning and Work Experience Policy Learning activities
